goal: Generate and validate a vtk.js streamline scene with browser evidence
title: vtk.js Scene Validation
abstract: Generate vtk.js code for the requested scene while preserving a deterministic browser-validation path. Scene kind: streamline. Use these retrieval-backed patterns: Use slice, volume, streamline, and mag-iso as the benchmark backbone for vtk.js eval. | Expect screenshot, console log, page error log, and a structured render verdict. | Treat browser evidence as the acceptance contract for vtk.js scene validation. | Seed streamline scenes explicitly and confirm vector-field inputs before validation. | Build the scene in source -> mapper -> actor -> renderer order. | Always call renderer.resetCamera() before renderWindow.render().. Avoid regressions captured by these failure-fix hints: Capture artifact expectations per benchmark case so later eval output stays comparable. | If artifacts are missing, rerun the canonical Docker workflow instead of improvising a different browser command. | If streamline output is empty, inspect seed generation and pipeline ordering before general browser repair. | If validation fails early, retry with the minimal full-screen bootstrap scene first. | When the page says vtk is not defined, restore the runtime script include before changing scene logic. | If the canvas is missing, reduce the page to a known-good bootstrap scene and compare new artifacts.. Acceptance contract: The scene should create a visible canvas during browser verification. | The script should end with renderer.resetCamera() followed by renderWindow.render(). | Browser evidence should include render-verification.json, browser-console.json, page-errors.json, and render-screenshot.png. | The browser console and page error logs should stay free of runtime errors. | The generated streamline workflow should make seed generation and vector-field wiring explicit..
